details.description: A dramatic ground-up view of the Burj Khalifa against a clear, slightly hazy blue sky. The skyscraper’s reflective glass-and-aluminum cladding gleams in the sun, and its stacked, tapering setbacks form a telescoping silhouette culminating in a slender spire. The façade’s vertical mullions and banded mechanical floors are visible. Lush landscaping frames the base, with date-palm fronds entering from the right and left and low shrubs at the bottom edge, suggesting a park or promenade setting near the tower. Daylight is bright and crisp, typical of Dubai’s climate, with no other buildings prominently visible, emphasizing the tower’s height and neo‑futurist character.

entities.name: Burj Khalifa
entities.notes: World’s tallest building (828 m), opened 2010; developed by Emaar; design by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (lead designer Adrian Smith); clad in glass, aluminum, and stainless steel; buttressed-core structural system; inspired in plan by the Hymenocallis flower.

entities.name: Burj Khalifa
entities.notes: Mixed-use supertall skyscraper comprising offices, residences, Armani Hotel, and observation decks (“At the Top”). Address: 1 Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Blvd, Downtown Dubai.

entities.name: Neo-futurism (high-tech skyscraper expression)
entities.notes: Sleek glass-and-metal skin, aerodynamic tapering, and advanced engineering typical of neo‑futurist/high‑tech design; incorporates Islamic-inspired, tri-lobed plan geometry.

entities.name: Date palm (Phoenix dactylifera) landscaping
entities.notes: Mature palm fronds visible framing the tower, characteristic of Downtown Dubai’s streetscapes.

entities.notes: Master-planned district by Emaar encompassing Burj Khalifa, Dubai Fountain, and Dubai Mall.

details.description: A low-angle daytime photograph of the Burj Khalifa rising into a clear blue sky. The tower features its signature glass-facade with stepped setbacks and a slender spire reaching upward. In the foreground, a large palm tree on the right curves into the frame, with shrubs at the base, suggesting a landscaped urban park setting. The overall scene conveys Downtown Dubai near a public open space, with bright direct sunlight producing reflections on the building’s surface and a crisp, uninterrupted skyline.

details.description: A low-angle, vertical photograph capturing the Burj Khalifa in Dubai against a clear, vibrant blue sky. The skyscraper dominates the frame, showcasing its iconic neo-futurist architecture with a series of spiraling setbacks and a sleek glass-and-aluminum façade that tapers toward the spire. A bright sun glint reflects off the building's surface, creating a starburst effect. In the foreground, the right side of the image is framed by the green fronds of a large date palm tree, while additional foliage is visible at the bottom, suggesting the photo was taken from a nearby park or garden setting.

details.description: A low-angle vertical shot of the Burj Khalifa, the world's tallest building, rising into a clear, bright blue sky. The skyscraper's iconic tiered, tapering design is prominent, with its reflective glass and aluminum facade catching the sunlight. In the foreground, the large, green fronds of a palm tree frame the right side of the composition, while dense green foliage and tree tops are visible at the base. The perspective emphasizes the immense height and architectural complexity of the structure.

details.description: A ground-level perspective of the Burj Khalifa in Downtown Dubai, rising tall against a clear blue sky. The glass-clad tower features its distinctive tiered, stepped design with multiple setbacks as it narrows toward a spire. A palm tree frames the right side of the image, with low shrubs at the base, emphasizing the contrast between lush foreground vegetation and ultramodern skyline architecture.

details.description: Worm’s‑eye view of the Burj Khalifa, the world’s tallest skyscraper in Dubai. The tower’s stepped, telescoping form with stacked setbacks and a slender spire rises against a clear blue sky. Its reflective glass-and-metal curtain wall glints in the sun. At the base are landscaped plantings and prominent palm fronds framing the scene, indicating a ground-level vantage within the Downtown Dubai precinct near the tower’s podium.

details.description: A towering glass-clad skyscraper rises from the ground, tapering in vertical tiers to a slim spire. The Burj Khalifa dominates the frame with a highly reflective facade of blue-silver glass arranged in stacked, stepped sections. The sky is clear and blue, and foreground landscaping includes a palm tree on the right and low shrubs at the base, suggesting a public or park-like area in Downtown Dubai. The overall scene conveys a modern, ultra-tall urban landmark in a desert city.

details.description: A low-angle, vertical photograph capturing the full height of the Burj Khalifa in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, set against a clear, cloudless blue sky. The skyscraper dominates the composition, showcasing its distinctive tiered, spiraling design with setbacks that taper toward a sharp central spire. The facade is clad in reflective glass and aluminum, with a bright sun glare reflecting off the left-center portion of the structure. In the foreground, the scene is framed by lush greenery; a large palm tree with spreading fronds occupies the right side, while other leafy trees obscure the very base of the tower, suggesting the perspective is from the landscaped gardens or park surrounding the building.
